record.field: EverShop is a modern, TypeScript-first open-source ecommerce platform built with GraphQL, React and Node.js for developers who want full control over their online store.

EverShop is a modern, TypeScript-first open-source ecommerce platform built with GraphQL, React and Node.js for developers who want full control over their online store.

It ships the essential commerce building blocks out of the box — catalog, customer accounts, cart and checkout, order management, CMS and promotions — on a modular architecture where both the storefront and the admin panel can be extended or replaced. Its GraphQL API and clean separation of concerns make it equally suited to monolithic and headless setups, and it deploys in minutes via Docker, avoiding the vendor lock-in of hosted SaaS platforms.

Key features
  • Catalog management with products, categories, attributes and inventory
  • Cart, checkout and order management (OMS) with fulfillment and returns
  • Customer accounts, authentication and promotions / discount rules
  • GraphQL API and modular extension system for custom backend logic
  • React + TailwindCSS storefront with a customizable theme system
  • Built-in CMS for pages, blocks and widgets; first-class Docker deployment
Best for

Developers and agencies building tailored, self-hosted online stores who prefer a code-driven, extensible TypeScript stack over closed SaaS or heavyweight PHP platforms — ideal for both classic and headless commerce projects.

services: evershop-app: image: evershop/evershop:latest restart: always environment: DB_HOST: database DB_PORT: {{ db_port }} DB_PASSWORD: {{ db_password }} DB_USER: {{ db_user }} DB_NAME: {{ db_name }} depends_on: - database ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:3000' container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network database: image: postgres:16 restart: unless-stopped volumes: - ./postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data environment: POSTGRES_PASSWORD: {{ db_password }} POSTGRES_USER: {{ db_user }} POSTGRES_DB: {{ db_name }} container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_database' networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR docker compose exec -T database sh -c 'pg_dump -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura docker compose up -d database for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T database sh -c 'pg_isready -U "$POSTGRES_USER"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T database sh -c 'pg_restore -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T database sh -c "psql -U \$POSTGRES_USER -d \$POSTGRES_DB -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0
